violations.text: The door to the outside, located in the back, is not tight-fitting. Door sweep is observed worn down, door sweep to be replaced. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2025
The grout next to the prep unit is observed in poor repair. Pooling water and old food debris observed in worn down grout. Grout to be repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 10/20/2025

The presence of flying insects is not controlled as evidenced by numerous flying insects observed. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/08/2025

The door to the outside, located in the back, is not tight-fitting. Door sweep is observed worn down, door sweep to be replaced. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2025
The grout next to the prep unit is observed in poor repair. Pooling water and old food debris observed in worn down grout. Grout to be repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2025
A dozen muffins were observed in contact with flying insects. Muffins were disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/04/2025

The presence of flying insects is not controlled as evidenced by large accumulation of flying insects. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 10/04/2025

The door to the outside, located in the back, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. -This violation has not exceeded the original correction date of 7/08/25. New Violation. Correct By: 07/08/2025

The cutting boards on the sand-wich are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. -This violation has not exceeded the original correction date of 7/15/25. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025
No person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was (0) parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be (150-350) ppm. -Upon further inspection, sanitizer container observed empty. After replacing the sanitizer, the concentration of sanitizer observed at 200-400ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025

The walk-in cooler was observed held at 47-50 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. All TCS foods above 41 degrees Fahrenheit were voluntarily disposed of by staff. -The door observed not tightly fitted at the time of this inspection. -Establishment shall not store TCS foods in the walk-in cooler until it can consistently maintain an ambient temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025

The door to the outside, located in the back, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 07/08/2025

The cutting boards on the sandwich station are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025
Ice buildup observed in the walk-in freezer. Equipment shall be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2025

Plumbing underneath the 3 bay sink observed with water leakage. In addition, water leakage observed underneath the coffee machines area. Equipment shall be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2025
The grout in the front prep area was observed to be lifting/missing. New Violation. Correct By: 08/29/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety with RIDOH Center for Food Protection at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2024

The door to the outside, located in the back prep area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2024

The following was observed: 1) Floor tiles in front of walk-in cooler observed to be cracked/missing grout 2) Coving next to mop sink observed to be cracked The physical facilities must be in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/09/2024
